summary: Red-team proposal for the L47 qv6_007 claim-preservation gate: keep the existing L47 outputs as exposed diagnostic evidence only, stress the deterministic marker gate for false positives/false negatives, and block any generalization claim from qv6_007 diagnostics.

models.review_method: Read-only local artifact and source inspection; no paid API calls and no protected file edits.

outputs.description: Worker B proposal contract for L47 claim-gate red review.
Human-readable red-team proposal for edge cases, false positives, verification commands, and diagnostic-only labeling.

accepted_fields_or_changes.proposal: Accept the L47 claim-preservation checker as a no-API diagnostic gate for the exposed qv6_007 case only.
accepted_fields_or_changes.evidence: Existing L47 analysis shows L37 claim_preservation_rate=0.0 and L40 claim_preservation_rate=0.5; both gate_status=fail and claim_scope=diagnostic_only_exposed_case.

accepted_fields_or_changes.proposal: Label L40 as partial recovery: entity binding and unmanned-operation/HW linkage improved, physical access-control remains source-supported but underanswered.
accepted_fields_or_changes.evidence: L40 and L44 details have all-Goyang retrieval and judge 5/5, yet the access-control claim still fails the deterministic L47 gate.

accepted_fields_or_changes.proposal: A failed claim gate should block report-ready promotion for the exposed case, but must not create a new performance score or alter the strict scoreboard row.

Because scripts/check_claim_preservation.py returns exit code 1 when claims fail, orchestrators should treat a parsed JSON with gate_status=fail as an expected diagnostic gate failure, not as runner infrastructure failure.

rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The qv6_007 diagnostics are exposed targeted retries and should not be described as generalization, holdout, or broad v6 performance evidence.
L44 judge 5/5 is useful evidence of judge blindness on this pair; it is not evidence that qv6_007 is fixed.
A 2-claim exposed-case preservation rate is too narrow to graph beside EDD or claim as system-level claim-preservation quality.
Broad paid v7/v8/v9 runs remain premature until claim-preservation gate behavior is reviewed on false-positive/false-negative examples.

needs_orchestrator_review: Decide whether L47 should update loop_points/checkpoints after reviewing Worker B; this worker did not edit those protected or orchestration files.

Review whether source_search in eval/claim_preservation_expectations.json is too broad because scripts/check_claim_preservation.py currently loads whole matching data files, not a project-scoped row/span.
Review whether false_friend_hits should affect pass/fail status instead of being recorded only as evidence.
Review whether future details.json should include retrieved chunk text or stable row/chunk IDs so claim support is not inferred from global source-file markers.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: source_text_for() can append an entire data_list.csv if required_any appears anywhere in the file; source_any markers may then come from a different row or project.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: Block or warn unless source hits are within a project-scoped span matching target_org and target_biz, or within the retrieved chunk IDs for the answer.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: answer_any is marker-based. An answer can mention an access-control term while saying it is not confirmed.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: A negative/not-found statement near a claim anchor should fail even if answer_any has a lexical hit.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: L40 distinguishes account/access-permission security from physical access-control devices. The checker records false_friend_hits but does not currently use them in status.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: If false_friend_hits are present and physical answer markers are absent or negated, keep fail/source_supported_but_answer_underanswered.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: has_underanswer_near() uses a 220-character window. Longer answers can place the anchor and the not-confirmed phrase outside that window.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: Use section-level or sentence-level polarity around the claim, not only a fixed character window.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: The expectation markers are mojibake-sensitive. A future normalized Korean answer/source or slightly different spacing can become a false fail.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: Store stable canonical claim IDs and source row/chunk IDs, and keep marker lists as secondary evidence rather than the only proof.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: When source_supported is false and answer_supported is false, the current per-claim status can be pass even for must-preserve expectations.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: For required diagnostic claims, missing source evidence should produce blocked_expectation_or_source_gap, not pass.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: Some L38-era saved details predate answer_quality_issues; recompute scripts can backfill, but stale detail files can silently lack diagnostics.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: Verification should report detail_schema_version or explicitly note whether recomputed answer_quality_diagnostics are present.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: If details do not contain qv6_007, the gate should not look like a clean pass.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: answer_found=false should be a blocked or failed case for required exposed diagnostics.

red_team_edge_cases.why_it_matters: A future answer can be tuned to include the exact two expected markers while still omitting other canonical qv6_006 details.
red_team_edge_cases.expected_gate_behavior: Keep L47 as a narrow gate and require additional fresh or broader metamorphic pairs before making any general claim.

false_positive_traps.trap: Global CSV support from wrong project
false_positive_traps.test: Create or select a source row where the access-control marker appears under another Goyang-like project; the qv6_007 expectation should not pass source_supported from that row.

false_positive_traps.trap: System account access mistaken for physical access control
false_positive_traps.test: Use an answer that only mentions permissions, access logs, and network access control; it should not satisfy physical_access_control_system.
Use an answer saying physical access-control system is not confirmed while repeating the marker; it should fail by polarity.
Compare L44 judge 5/5 with L47 claim_gate fail; promotion logic should prefer the claim-gate caveat for report-ready status.
After any claim-gate run, aggregate summary should still have scoreboard_rows=1 and diagnostic_only rows should contain L38-L46/L47 style exposed diagnostics only.

verification_commands_proposed.purpose: Reproduce L37 exposed-case claim gate; nonzero exit means gate failed as expected, not script crash if JSON is written.

verification_commands_proposed.expected_result: summary.gate_status=fail, claims_failed=1, claim_preservation_rate=0.5; unmanned_operation_program passes and physical_access_control_system fails

verification_commands_proposed.purpose: Verify L47 did not promote exposed diagnostics into the scoreboard after aggregation.
verification_commands_proposed.expected_result: summary/summary.json keeps only L37 in scoreboard_rows; any claim-gate artifacts remain diagnostic-only or outside EDD rows

verification_commands_proposed.purpose: Verify existing L47 result labels directly without rerunning the checker.
Verify no paid calls are needed for the claim gate.
verification_commands_proposed.command: Review scripts/check_claim_preservation.py imports and execution path; it reads details, expectations, and local source text only.
verification_commands_proposed.expected_result: No OpenAI client, no RAGPipeline call, no run_eval call

allowed_language: L47 confirms a no-API diagnostic gate can catch the exposed qv6_007 residual underanswer.
L40 is a partial recovery over L37 on qv6_007: one of two diagnostic claims now passes.
The qv6_007 claim gate remains failed, so broad paid loops should stay closed until the gate and false-positive cases are reviewed.
disallowed_language: judge 5/5 confirms the answer is complete
result_labeling.scoreboard_policy: Do not add claim-gate rows to EDD scoreboard. Record claim-gate status in diagnostic summaries, loop_points, or report-ready gates only.

recommended_next_low_cost_steps: Add a no-API false-positive fixture pack for check_claim_preservation.py before broad paid runs.
Narrow source_search to retrieved chunk IDs or source row spans for qv6_007.
Make false_friend_hits affect status for the physical_access_control_system claim.
Add a gate_result_label field to any L47 summary: diagnostic_only_exposed_case, not_generalization_evidence.
Use L44 as a report example of judge blindness only after pairing it with the L47 gate failure.

observed_results: No paid API/model calls were made.
git status --short reported this workspace is not a git repository from I:/0706/rfp-rag-project, so git hygiene could not be used as verification.
L37 is the only scoreboard row in summary/summary.json; L38-L46 are diagnostic-only or dry-run rows.
L40 and L44 both show all-Goyang retrieval for qv6_007 and judge 5/5, but both still underanswer physical access control.
Existing L47 analysis for L37 has gate_status=fail, claims_failed=2, claim_preservation_rate=0.0, claim_scope=diagnostic_only_exposed_case.
Existing L47 analysis for L40 has gate_status=fail, claims_failed=1, claim_preservation_rate=0.5, claim_scope=diagnostic_only_exposed_case.
scripts/check_claim_preservation.py is local/no-API and returns nonzero when claim failures exist.
Potential red risks found: broad source-file search, marker/polarity fragility, false_friend_hits not used in status, stale detail schema handling, and overclaiming a 2-claim exposed diagnostic rate.
